macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Ends Five-Week Losing Streak with 3% Gain Amid Mideast Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 rose over 3% last week, halting a five-week decline for the first time since 2022, despite soaring oil prices from the Middle East conflict. U.S. 10-year Treasury yields fell to 4.31% while Brent crude climbed toward $112 per barrel.[1][3]

items.title: Oil Prices Surge Past $100/Barrel as Strait of Hormuz Shutdown Disrupts Supply
items.summary: Brent crude hit $109.37 per barrel amid escalating Persian Gulf war, with spot prices reaching $141.26, echoing 1970s oil crises and straining global energy supply. The shutdown has pulled significant oil off the market, boosting inflation fears.[5][6]

items.title: Strategists Warn Fed Ignoring War Risks, Predict Rate Hikes as Inflation Heads to 3.5%
items.summary: Markets, economy, and Fed are overlooking Middle East conflict impacts, with inflation trending away from 2% target toward 3.5% per upcoming CPI data. Labor market remains resilient with 178k jobs added in March and unemployment at 4.3%.[2]

items.title: Upcoming US March CPI, Fed Minutes, and Consumer Confidence in Focus
items.summary: Friday's March CPI data expected after February's 2.4% YoY, with Fed minutes to signal rate path amid oil volatility from Middle East war. Consumer confidence hit historically low 53.3 in March, tied to energy cost spikes.[4]

items.title: Mideast Conflict Fuels Energy Security Investments, AI Power Demand
items.summary: Middle East shock and AI-driven power needs are accelerating energy infrastructure investments, creating opportunities in energy, commodities, and defense. Governments prioritize supply chain resilience amid elevated oil prices.[1]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: TRU Surges 98% as Top Crypto Gainer Amid High Volatility
items.summary: TRU led 24-hour gainers with a 98.46% surge to $0.0111 on $177.78M volume, followed by RED (+65.80%) and PCI (+27.12%). ARIAIP was the biggest loser, down 49.24% to $0.0037.

items.title: Bitcoin Dips to $69K as Market Cap Falls 1% to $2.43T
items.summary: Bitcoin fell 1% to around $69,100 after hitting $70,200, with Solana down 2.8% to $80.18 and XRP down 2.5% to $1.32 amid broader market decline.

items.title: $170M in Crypto Liquidations Led by Bitcoin and Ethereum
items.summary: Futures liquidations hit $169.8M in 24 hours, primarily Bitcoin and Ethereum positions, amid market turbulence and shifting macroeconomic indicators.

items.title: Over 50% of XRP Supply Underwater After Price Drop to $1.33
items.summary: Only 43.4% of XRP's circulating supply is profitable, with $1.68M in shorts and $298K in longs liquidated; XRP loses fourth-largest spot to BNB.

items.title: Zcash Outperforms Bitcoin with 5% Gain on Record Shielded Supply
items.summary: Zcash rose nearly 5% to $268 amid market dip, driven by record 5.18M shielded ZEC (31% of supply worth $1.38B), topping Bitcoin's performance.

items.title: Crypto Market Cap Dips 0.6% to $2.35T, Lingering Near 50-Day Average
items.summary: Overall crypto market capitalization fell 0.6% in the past 24 hours to $2.35 trillion, showing no rush to recover.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Cuts OpenClaw Access from Claude Subscriptions
items.summary: Anthropic has ended OpenClaw access in Claude subscriptions amid a global token crunch, offering credits to affected users for transition. Chinese AI rivals are clashing over the exit.

items.title: Foxconn Reports Record Q1 Sales Driven by AI Server Demand
items.summary: Foxconn achieved its highest-ever first-quarter sales of USD 66.62 billion, fueled by surging demand for AI servers.

items.title: Iran Threatens to Destroy OpenAI’s $30B Stargate Data Center in UAE
items.summary: Iran has threatened to target OpenAI's massive Stargate AI data center under construction in Abu Dhabi, valued at $30 billion.

items.title: OpenAI Advocates for Robot Taxes and Public Wealth Fund to Offset AI Job Losses
items.summary: OpenAI is calling for robot taxes and a public wealth fund to mitigate the economic impacts of AI-driven job displacement.

items.title: YouTube Creators Allege Amazon Scrapes Videos for AI Training
items.summary: YouTube creators claim Amazon is scraping their videos to train AI models, raising concerns over content usage without permission.

items.title: Google Cloud Launches Conversational Analytics for Looker Embedded
items.summary: Google Cloud has introduced Conversational Analytics, now available for Looker Embedded environments to enhance AI-driven data insights.

items.title: ASML Breakthrough in Chip Tech to Meet AI Demand
items.summary: ASML's advanced extreme ultraviolet lithography system etches 8-nanometer features, enabling 2.9 times more transistors per chip to support AI infrastructure growth.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie's Marathon Struggles with Declining Player Counts and Support Issues
items.summary: Marathon peaked at 88,000 players a month ago but is now declining sharply, diverting resources from Destiny 2 and facing balancing issues, with speculation Sony may shut it down.
items.source: YouTube - Marathon Is Dying And It's NOT Looking Good For Bungie

items.title: Super Mario Galaxy Movie Achieves Record-Breaking $350M Global Opening
items.summary: The Super Mario Galaxy movie becomes the only animated series to open globally over $350 million twice, marking another blockbuster for Universal and Illumination.

items.title: Take-Two Lays Off Entire AI Team Amid Industry Shifts
items.summary: Take-Two Interactive has laid off its entire AI team, as discussed in recent gaming news alongside hardware price hikes and game reveals.

items.title: State of Decay 3 Reveal Trailer Criticized as Misleading by Developers
items.summary: The State of Decay 3 trailer is called 'basically fake' after a developer dashes hopes for features like zombie deer.

items.title: Unity Gaming Report 2026 Highlights AI, Small Games, and Industry Trends
items.summary: The 2026 Unity Gaming Report emphasizes key shifts including AI integration, the rise of small games, and tips for game developers in 2026.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung Forecasts Massive Q1 2026 Profit Surge
items.summary: Samsung reported an 8x profit increase in Q1 2026, driven by booming AI data center demand and surging memory prices. The company anticipates insane operating profit growth due to high memory chip demand.[1]

items.title: Samsung Discontinues Messages App for Google Messages
items.summary: Samsung is retiring its Messages app in favor of Google Messages across devices. This shift standardizes messaging on Android's default app.[1]

items.title: Netflix Launches Playground Kids Games App
items.summary: Netflix introduced Playground, a standalone games app for children aged eight and under. It expands Netflix's gaming offerings beyond streaming.[1]

items.title: Google Pixel 10a Launches in Japan with Exclusive Color
items.summary: Google released the Pixel 10a in Japan featuring an exclusive 'Isai Blue' color. The launch is limited to that market for now.[1]

items.title: Broadcom Secures Google as Customer for Future TPUs
items.summary: Broadcom's stock jumped after confirming Google as a customer for upcoming TPU generations. This deal bolsters Broadcom's position in custom chip manufacturing.[1]

items.title: Google Removes Play Store Review Filtering by Device
items.summary: Google discontinued the ability to filter Play Store reviews by specific device models, replacing it with a less useful alternative. Users can no longer easily check app compatibility feedback.[1]

items.title: Pixel Update Turns Some Devices into Bricks
items.summary: A recent software update has rendered certain Google Pixel phones unusable, with no response from Google yet. Affected users report devices as expensive paperweights.[1]

items.title: Five New Security Cameras Redefine Expectations
items.summary: New security cameras from various makers introduce advanced features that change standards for home and business surveillance. They offer improved detection and integration.[1]
